title: salix × fragilis f. vitellina
intro: Salix × fragilis f. vitellina, also known as the Weeping Willow or Salix Fragilis Vitellina, is a cultivar of the white willow tree (Salix alba), characterized by its slender branches and golden-yellow foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties derived from its salicin content. Consuming the bark of Salix × fragilis f. vitellina has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as treating rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is often used to prepare herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used both internally and externally for therapeutic purposes.
